```

Calable[activation] -> bool
```

methods.name: viewflow.workflow.nodes.switch.Switch.Default
documentation: Last node to activate if no one other succeed.

Gateway that selects one of the outgoing node.

Activates first node with matched condition.

Example:

```
select_responsible_person = (
    flow.Switch()
    .Case(this.dean_approval, lambda act: a.process.need_dean)
    .Case(this.head_approval, lambda act: a.process.need_head)
    .Default(this.supervisor_approval)
)
```

classes.name: viewflow.workflow.nodes.mixins.NodePermissionMixin

methods.name: viewflow.workflow.nodes.mixins.NodePermissionMixin.Permission
documentation:
Make task available for users with specific permission.

Accepts permissions name or callable :: Callable[Activation] -> string:

```
.Permission('my_app.can_approve')
.Permission(lambda process: 'my_app.department_manager_{}'.format(process.department.pk))
```

Task specific permission could be auto created during migration:

```
# Creates `process_class.can_do_task_process_class` permission
do_task = View().Permission(auto_create=True)

# You can specify permission codename and description right here
# The following creates `process_class.can_execute_task` permission
do_task = View().Permission('can_execute_task', help_text='Custom text', auto_create=True)
```

methods.name: viewflow.workflow.nodes.mixins.NodePermissionMixin._ready
documentation:
Insert additional flow permissions to the meta of the process model.

Permissions itself are created as usual during django database
migration process.
Node mixin to restrict access using django permissions.

classes.name: viewflow.workflow.context.Context
documentation:
Thread-local activation context, dynamically scoped.

```
keyword propagate_exception

If True, on activation failure
exception will be propagated to
previous activation. If False,
current task activation will be
marked as failed.
```

Usage

```
with Context(propagate_exception=False):
     print(context.propagate_exception)  # prints 'False'
print(context.propagate_exception)  # prints default 'True'
```

classes.name: viewflow.workflow.flow.nodes.Start
documentation:
The `Start` node in a flow.

This node is used as the initial step in a flow by a user.

[Live Demo](https://demo.viewflow.io/workflow/flows/helloworld/start/) /
[Cookbook sample](https://github.com/viewflow/cookbook/blob/main/workflow101/helloworld/flows.py)

```python
class MyFlow(flow.Flow):
    start = (
        flow.Start(views.CreateProcessView.as_view(fields=["text"]))
        .Annotation(title=_("New message"))
        .Permission(auto_create=True)
        .Next(this.approve)
    )

    ...
```

classes.name: viewflow.workflow.flow.nodes.StartHandle
documentation:
The `Start` handle node in a flow.

This node is used as the initial step in a flow from code

```python
class MyFlow(flow.Flow):
    start = flow.StartHandle(this.on_start_process).Next(this.approve)

    def start_process(self, activation, sample=False):
        activation.process.sample = sample
        return activation.process

    ...

process = MyFlow.start.run(sample=True)
```

classes.name: viewflow.workflow.flow.nodes.End
documentation:
The `End` node in a flow.

This node serves as the terminal point of a flow

```python
class MyFlow(flow.Flow):
    ...

    approved = this.End()
    rejected = this.End()
```

classes.name: viewflow.workflow.flow.nodes.View

methods.name: viewflow.workflow.flow.nodes.View.assign_view
documentation: View for a task assign.

methods.name: viewflow.workflow.flow.nodes.View.unassign_view
documentation: View for a task assign.

class_variables.name: viewflow.workflow.flow.nodes.View.revive_view_class
documentation: Execute View

Represents a user-interaction node within a flow

```python
class MyFlow(flow.Flow):
    ...

    approve = (
        flow.View(views.UpdateProcessView.as_view(fields=["approved"]))
        .Annotation(
            title=_("Approve"),
            description=_("Supervisor approvement"),
            summary_template=_("Message review required"),
            result_template=_(
                "Message was {{ process.approved|yesno:'Approved,Rejected' }}"
            ),
        )
        .Permission(auto_create=True)
        .Next(this.check_approve)
    )

    ...
```

classes.name: viewflow.workflow.flow.nodes.If
documentation:
The If-gate

```python
   class MyFlow(flow.Flow):
       ...

       check_approve = (
           flow.If(lambda activation: activation.process.approved)
           .Annotation(title=_("Approvement check"))
           .Then(this.send)
           .Else(this.end)
       )

.       ...
```

classes.name: viewflow.workflow.flow.nodes.Handle
documentation:
Represents a task executed from the other parts of code

Usage:
To define a handle in a flow and run it:

```python
class MyFlow(flow.Flow):
    ...
    my_handle = flow.Handle().Next(this.join_gate)

task = task=process.task_set.get(flow_task=MyFlow.my_handle, status=STATUS.NEW),
MyFlow.my_handle.run(task)
```

```
param token

str
```

methods.name: viewflow.workflow.token.Token.is_split_token
documentation: True, if it is a token of parallel task.

methods.name: viewflow.workflow.token.Token.get_base_split_token
documentation: Return token before last split happens.

methods.name: viewflow.workflow.token.Token.get_common_split_prefix
documentation: Common prefix for tokens.

methods.name: viewflow.workflow.token.Token.split_token_source
documentation: Span a set of uniq tokens with common prefix.

Helper for tree-like flow token management.

We follow basic strategy for flow split/join

Flow starts with only one 'start' token,

- each split adds split_pk and path id to following task 'start/3_4',
  and 'start/3_4/7_4' on one more split

- each join removes corresponding split token addition, so 'start/3_4/7_4'
  becomes 'start/3_4'

modules.name: viewflow.workflow.lock
documentation: Prevents inconsistent db updates for flow.

classes.name: viewflow.workflow.lock.NoLock
documentation:
No pessimistic locking, just execute flow task in transaction.

Not suitable when you have Join nodes in your flow.

classes.name: viewflow.workflow.lock.SelectForUpdateLock
documentation:
Database lock uses `select ... for update` on the process instance row.

Recommended to use with PostgreSQL.

classes.name: viewflow.workflow.lock.CacheLock
documentation:
Task lock based on Django cache.

Use it if primary cache backend has transactional `add` functionality,
like `memcached`.

Example:

```
class MyFlow(Flow):
    lock_impl = CacheLock(cache=caches['locks'])
```

The example uses a different cache. The default cache
is Django `default` cache configuration.

All Rights Reserved.

functions.name: viewflow.fsm.chart.chart
documentation:
Draws a directed graph (digraph) of the state transitions defined in the
given `flow_state`.

Args:
    flow_state (StateDescriptor): The state descriptor object.
    exclude_guards (bool): Whether to exclude transitions with no target state.

Returns:
    str: A string representation of the digraph in the DOT language.

The function uses the `flow_state` object to extract the transitions and the
states involved. It then generates a DOT language string, which can be used
with tools like Graphviz to produce an image of the graph.

Note that the function ignores the `State.USE_RETURN_VALUE` and `State.SELECT`
states for now, as these are not supported yet.

modules.name: viewflow.fsm

classes.name: viewflow.workflow.flow.viewset.FlowAppViewset

class_variables.name: viewflow.workflow.flow.viewset.FlowAppViewset.base_template_name
documentation: Inbox

Viewset includes flow as an separate App into Site.

[Cookbook sample](https://github.com/viewflow/cookbook/blob/main/workflow101/config/urls.py)

Usage:

```python
site = Site(
    viewsets=[
        FlowAppViewset(
            ShipmentFlow,
            icon="local_shipping",
            viewsets=[
                ShipmentCRUDViewset()
            ]
    ]
)

urlpatterns = [path("", site.urls)]
```

class_variables.name: viewflow.workflow.flow.viewset.NestedFlowsApp.icon
documentation: Permissions

classes.name: viewflow.workflow.flow.viewset.WorkflowAppViewset
documentation:
Viewset includes multiples flow with common Inbox/Queue/Archive views as an
separate App into Site.

[Life demo](https://demo.viewflow.io/workflow/inbox/)

Usage:

```python
site = Site(
    viewsets=[
        WorkflowAppViewset(
            flow_viewsets=[
                FlowViewset(HelloWorldFlow, icon="assignment"),
                FlowViewset(ShipmentFlow, icon="local_shipping"),
                FlowViewset(DynamicSplitFlow, icon="tune"),
            ]
    ]
)

urlpatterns = [path("", site.urls)]
```

Extract keyword arguments from a Django path expression, which are used as

Example Usage:

classes.name: viewflow.utils.LazySingletonDescriptor
documentation:
Descriptor class that creates a lazy singleton instance.

This descriptor can be used as a class attribute, and the first time the
attribute is accessed, it creates an instance of the class. Subsequent
accesses return the same instance, effectively making the class a singleton.

```
from material.contrib.admin import Admin

]
```

classes.name: viewflow.urls.base.Viewset

methods.name: viewflow.urls.base.Viewset._get_urls
documentation: Collect URLs from the instance attributes.

Declarative, class-based django routing configuration

An Viewset class automatically collect URL patterns from class attributes
with names ends on `_url` and includes nested Viewset classes from
attributes with names ends on '_urls'.

Viewset classes could be inherited, extended, and have overridden attributes.

classes.name: viewflow.this_object.This

The rationale is ability to specify references to the class
attributes and methods before they are declared.

`this` is like a `self` but for the class body.

classes.name: viewflow.forms.renderers.FormLayout
documentation: Default form layout.

classes.name: viewflow.forms.renderers.Layout
documentation: Customizable form layout.

classes.name: viewflow.forms.renderers.LayoutNode
documentation: Base class for self-rendered nodes.

classes.name: viewflow.forms.renderers.Column
documentation:
Place elements vertically stacked, one under another.

Example:
    layout = Layout(
        Row(
            Column('first_name', 'last_name', desktop=8, tablet=6)
            'sex_options'
        )
    )

classes.name: viewflow.forms.renderers.Row
documentation:
Spread elements over a single line.

Example:

    layout = Layout(
        Row(
            'first_name',
            Row('last_name', 'sex', tablet=5)
        )
    )

classes.name: viewflow.forms.renderers.Span
documentation:
Span a form field over several columns.

Example::

: layout = Layout(

  : Row(Span('first_name'), Span('last_name'))
    Row(

    > Span('email', tablet=6, mobile=3),
    > 'sex'

    )

  )

By default span is auto-sized. On a desktop all auto-sized elements
would be spread equally over the free place of a row, non occupied by
elements with specific sizes.

On mobile and tablet if all elements in a row have auto-sizes,
each element would be placed in a new line. If even one element
in a row has a specific size, all auto-sized elements would be
kept in a single line, like on a desktop.
